title: thelypteris quelpartensis var. yaku-montana
intro: Thelypteris quelpartensis var. yaku-montana, also known as the Japanese shield fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. It is a medicinal plant that has been traditionally used in East Asian medicine, particularly in Japan and Korea, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of T. quelpartensis var. yaku-montana include its ability to reduce fever, relieve headaches, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues.
